mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market strength that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive payroll signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) acceleration combined with housing starts (HOUST) above 1,200 thousand units signals robust consumer and construction demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 1,200 thousand
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with synchronized consumer and housing strength
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST conditions are met, or interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline below 109.0 signals supply-side weakness that constrains corporate output and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO < 109.0, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rise above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 4.0%, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) acceleration above 3.5% combined with elevated federal funds rate above 3.0% signals persistent monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where both inflation and rates conditions are met, or interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,784.632 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2024-04-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 110.0 combined with payroll employment growth above 150,000 signals synchronized supply and labor expansion that sustains corporate capacity and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 110.0 AND PAYEMS monthly growth > 150,000 persons
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where both INDPRO and PAYEMS conditions are met, or interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.
